sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Sensata Technologies (NYSE:ST) Price Target Raised to $42.00 at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for Sensata Technologies (NYSE:ST) from $37.00 to $42.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 4.61% from the stock's current price. Other analysts have also adjusted price targets, with an average "Hold" rating and a target price of $41.90 for the company.

feed.title: Grupo Supervielle (SUPV) Projected to Post Quarterly Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: Grupo Supervielle (SUPV) is anticipated to report its Q1 2026 earnings on Wednesday, May 6th, with analysts forecasting an EPS of $0.03 and revenues of $198.04 million. The company's stock is currently trading around $8.61, reflecting a mixed market view and several recent analyst downgrades. SUPV has a negative P/E ratio and an average analyst rating of "Hold" with a price target of $13.00.

feed.title: Polen Capital Adds Rollins Inc Position Amid Market Volatility
feed.summary: Polen Capital initiated a new position in Rollins, Inc. (NYSE:ROL) during the first quarter of 2026, despite its Focus Growth Strategy experiencing a decline in a volatile market. The investment firm highlighted Rollins' consistent growth, high customer retention, and significant expansion opportunities in the fragmented pest control industry. Rollins, Inc. maintains a market capitalization of $26.84 billion and analysts see potential for future growth under its new CEO.

feed.title: Yum Brands wants AI and beverages to supercharge growth
feed.summary: Yum Brands plans to leverage artificial intelligence through its Byte system and introduce new menu innovations, particularly in beverages, to attract more customers and boost growth. The company is implementing AI-powered dynamic menu boards at Taco Bell and using a "global innovation pantry" at KFC to share successful limited-time offers and menu items across markets. While Taco Bell and The Habit Burger Grill saw sales growth, KFC and Pizza Hut experienced declines, indicating a need for these new strategies.

feed.title: Fund Update: 327,387 INTUIT (INTU) shares added to FLOSSBACH VON STORCH SE portfolio
feed.summary: FLOSSBACH VON STORCH SE has increased its holdings in INTUIT (INTU) by adding 327,387 shares to its portfolio, as reported in a recent SEC 13F filing for the Q1 2026 period. The article also details significant institutional investor activity, insider trading, congressional stock trading, and current analyst ratings and price targets for INTU stock.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials beats revenue estimates on strong demand for construction materials By Reuters
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue estimates due to robust demand for its construction materials, including crushed stone, sand, and gravel. The company's shares rose 4% as increased spending on public infrastructure, data centers, and energy projects boosted sales. Total revenue climbed 7.4% to $1.76 billion, exceeding expectations, and adjusted profit per share also saw a significant increase.

feed.title: UBS reiterates Buy on Sysco stock on volume growth improvement
feed.summary: UBS has reiterated a Buy rating and a $90.00 price target on Sysco (NYSE:SYY) due to continued operational improvements in the third quarter. Sysco reported a strong US local case volume growth of 3.3% and anticipates reaching its full-period goal of 2.5%+ local case growth for the fourth quarter. Despite missing some Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings estimates, analysts view the stock's current valuation as a favorable risk/reward profile.

feed.title: Uber adds hotel bookings as part of its goal to be the app for everything (UBER:NYSE)
feed.summary: Uber Technologies (UBER) has announced a new feature allowing users to book hotels through its app, expanding its services beyond ride-hailing and food delivery. This move is part of the company's broader strategy to become a comprehensive "app for everything." The hotel booking feature is facilitated through a partnership with Expedia Group (EXPE).

feed.title: Phillips 66 Beats Q1 Estimates by $0.88 Per Share as Refining Margins Surge
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) exceeded Q1 earnings estimates by $0.88 per share, reporting adjusted earnings of $0.49 per share against a consensus loss of $0.39. The strong performance, driven by soaring refining margins amid a Middle East conflict, resulted in a net income of $207 million. The company also highlighted expansions in its Sweeny NGL fractionation and Freeport LPG export capacities, alongside ongoing projects like the Coastal Bend NGL pipeline expansion and a proposed NGL fractionator in Corpus Christi.

feed.title: As DTE seeks $474 million rate hike, Nessel pledges to intervene
feed.summary: Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel is vowing to fight DTE Energy's latest request for a $474 million electrical rate increase for residential customers, which would be a 9.7% hike. Nessel, a frequent critic of the company, argues that DTE consistently seeks rate increases and treats Michigan families like an "open checkbook" to satisfy shareholders. She also expressed concerns about heavily redacted contracts related to a controversial data center project in Saline Township, which DTE claims will be key to a two-year rate increase pause if it comes online as planned.

feed.title: Marshalls store coming to Malone
feed.summary: Marshalls is set to open a new retail store in Malone, occupying the former Big Lots location. This move marks another expansion for TJX Companies, which operates several well-known retail chains. While an official opening date is yet to be confirmed, the company is actively seeking a store manager for the new location.

feed.title: Public Storage (PSA) FFO Near US$773m Tests Bullish Margin Resilience Narrative
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) reported Q1 2026 revenue of US$1.2 billion and FFO of US$772.7 million. While FFO remained stable, the net profit margin decreased from 37.4% to 35% year-over-year, challenging the bullish narrative of margin resilience. The article delves into bull and bear arguments, contrasting the company's discounted valuation and growth forecasts with concerns about declining earnings and high debt levels.

feed.title: Is Roper Technologies (ROP) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Weakness?
feed.summary: Roper Technologies (ROP) stock has seen significant declines over the past year, prompting an analysis of its current valuation. According to a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and Price/Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison, the stock appears to be undervalued by approximately 42.8% and also trades below its "Fair Ratio." Despite recent weakness tied to sector re-evaluation, the company's underlying value propositions suggest it might still be an attractive investment.

feed.title: A Look At CenterPoint Energy (CNP) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) has shown recent stock price momentum, closing at $43.13, with an 11.36% year-to-date total return. Despite being deemed 6.5% undervalued with a fair value of $46.13 and future EPS growth projections, its P/E ratio of 26.3x is higher than industry averages, suggesting potential valuation risk. Investors are encouraged to consider both the growth narrative and valuation risks, exploring the full picture before making investment decisions.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Maintains an “In Line” Rating on Lowe’s Companies, Inc. (LOW)
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has maintained an "In Line" rating for Lowe's Companies, Inc. (LOW), raising its price target to $250 from $245. This decision comes as Lowe's expands its partnership with Relex Solutions to integrate AI for inventory planning and restocking, aiming for improved efficiency and real-time decision-making, with a full rollout expected by early 2027.

feed.title: Vanguard reports 7.7% stake in Newmont (NEM) — 83.2M shares disclosed
feed.summary: Vanguard Capital Management has reported a 7.7% stake in Newmont

feed.title: Vulcan Materials beats revenue estimates on strong demand for construction materials
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials (VMC.N) surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue expectations, driven by robust demand for its construction materials like crushed stone, sand, and gravel. The building supplies maker's shares climbed 4% in morning trading as increased spending on public infrastructure, data centers, and energy projects fueled demand, despite rising input costs. The company reported a 7.4% rise in total revenue to $1.76 billion, exceeding anticipated figures, and its adjusted profit per share reached $1.26.

feed.title: Generac surges after raising full-year sales forecast on growing data center backlog (GNRC:NYSE)
feed.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. (GNRC) stock surged 14.5% to its highest intraday level in over three and a half years after beating Q1 adjusted earnings expectations. The company raised its full-year sales forecast, driven by strong demand for backup generators and a growing backlog, particularly from data centers. This positive outlook signals confidence in continued performance.

feed.title: Cooper Companies stock hits 52-week low at 61.76 USD
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (COO) recently hit a 52-week low of $61.76, marking a 23% year-to-date decline, although InvestingPro data suggests the stock is undervalued and in oversold territory, with management aggressively buying back shares. Despite this downturn, the company reported strong Q1 fiscal 2026 earnings, surpassing expectations and raising full-year guidance, leading analysts like Needham and Barclays to increase their price targets. These positive financial and strategic developments indicate potential for recovery despite current market pressures.

feed.title: Amex pushes for agentic standards
feed.summary: American Express is advocating for a shared industry standard in agentic commerce, demonstrating its commitment by offering consumer protections for bot-driven purchases. Despite rivals Visa and Mastercard also developing their own protocols, Amex emphasizes the need for collective interoperability to ensure trust, transparency, and accountability across the payments ecosystem. The company believes standardized practices are essential for the nascent agentic payments sector to grow and handle potential disputes effectively.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: IDEX beats Q1 2026 forecasts, stock jumps
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation reported strong Q1 2026 results, surpassing EPS and revenue forecasts, which led to a 7.21% stock surge in pre-market trading. The company raised its full-year EPS guidance for 2026, driven by robust performance in its Health & Science Technologies segment and strategic focus on advantage markets. Executives highlighted consistent innovation, effective capital deployment, and the strong backlog in HST as key factors for continued optimism despite some geopolitical uncertainties.

feed.title: Stanley Black & Decker anticipates higher tariffs later this year, cites ​inflation ​pressures from Iran conflict
feed.summary: Stanley Black & Decker is anticipating higher tariffs later this year, with Chief Financial Officer Pat Hallinan stating that underlying tariff costs will likely be similar to pre-Supreme Court ruling levels by August. The company is also experiencing inflationary pressures on resins, freight, battery metals, and tungsten due to the conflict in the Middle East. Despite these challenges, Stanley Black & Decker reported first-quarter net income of $59.6 million, exceeding Wall Street expectations, and has raised prices to offset increased costs.

feed.title: Uber moves toward becoming an 'everything app' with hotel bookings powered by Expedia
feed.summary: Uber is expanding its services by allowing users to book hotel rooms through its app, partnering with Expedia Group for listings. This move is part of Uber's strategy to become an "everything app," aiming to consolidate various customer needs into a single platform. The company plans to integrate over a million Vrbo vacation rentals later this year and offer discounts and Uber credits to Uber One members.

feed.title: TD (TD) launches auto-callable ETF-linked notes with ≥13.5% coupon — due 2029
feed.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering market-linked, auto-callable senior debt securities due May 30, 2029, tied to the performance of three State Street sector ETFs: Energy (XLE), Technology (XLK), and Health Care (XLV). These notes offer quarterly contingent coupon payments of at least 13.50% per annum if the lowest performing ETF is at or above 75% of its starting price. The securities are auto-callable if the lowest performing ETF reaches its starting price, and investors face potential principal loss if not called and the lowest performing ETF falls below 70% of its starting price at maturity.

feed.title: Fund Update: 3,291,764 SONY GROUP (SONY) shares added to Mondrian Investment Partners LTD portfolio
feed.summary: Mondrian Investment Partners LTD has increased its stake in Sony Group (SONY) by adding 3,291,764 shares to its portfolio, as per a new SEC 13F filing for the Q1 2026 reporting period. This move represents a 37.9% increase in their holdings, valued at approximately $68.1 million. The article also details other significant institutional investor activities in SONY and recent congressional trades involving the stock.

feed.title: Garmin Ltd. 2026 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation (NYSE:GRMN) 2026-04-29
feed.summary: Garmin Ltd. (NYSE:GRMN) has published its slide deck in conjunction with its Q1 2026 earnings call. This presentation provides details relevant to their first-quarter financial results. The article was written by SA Transcripts, Seeking Alpha's team dedicated to developing and publishing earnings call transcripts.

feed.title: TMUS Q1 Earnings Beat Estimates on Strong Service Revenue Growth
feed.summary: T-Mobile US, Inc. (TMUS) reported strong first-quarter 2026 earnings, beating analyst estimates with $2.70 per share and $23.11 billion in total revenues, driven primarily by robust service revenue growth and increased postpaid accounts. Despite higher operating expenses and UScellular-related costs impacting net income and operating income, the company demonstrated solid profitability, strong cash flow, and increased its full-year 2026 outlook for postpaid net account additions, core adjusted EBITDA, and adjusted free cash flow. This positive performance is attributed to continued customer momentum, ARPA expansion, and strategic network investments.

feed.title: IBM to Add 750 Jobs in AI, Quantum Computing at Chicago Tech Hub
feed.summary: IBM plans to add 750 jobs over the next five years at the Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park in Chicago, focusing on artificial intelligence, quantum computing, cybersecurity, and data science. The company will receive $19 million in state tax credits and establish the FutureNow Chicago center at the tech hub. This initiative deepens IBM's investment in the region and aligns with Illinois Governor JB Pritzker's support for technological advancements.

feed.title: Uber app adds hotel bookings through Expedia, with 700,000 properties planned
feed.summary: Uber Technologies announced new products and features at its GO-GET event, including a partnership with Expedia Group allowing users to book hotels directly within the Uber app, with plans to offer over 700,000 properties globally. The update aims to simplify travel logistics for users, with Uber One members receiving benefits like 10% back in credits, and will later integrate Vrbo vacation rentals. Additionally, Expedia will integrate Uber rides into its app, and Uber introduced other features like Travel Mode, global Uber One benefits, "Shop for Me," and AI-powered Voice Bookings.

feed.title: Franklin Resources (NYSE: ANDG) discloses 8.9% beneficial stake
feed.summary: Franklin Resources, Inc. and its affiliated investment managers have disclosed an 8.9% beneficial stake in Andersen Group Inc. (NYSE: ANDG) Class A common stock, totaling 1,130,323 shares, as of March 31, 2026. This ownership was reported in an amended Schedule 13G/A filing, noting that Franklin Advisers, Inc. holds sole voting and dispositive power over a majority of these shares. The filing also highlights an internal realignment within Franklin Resources and identifies Franklin Small Cap Growth Fund as holding a significant portion (7.5%) of the reported shares.

feed.title: Which Is the Better Small-Cap ETF, Vanguard's VB or State Street's SPSM?
feed.summary: This article compares two small-cap ETFs, Vanguard's VB and State Street's SPSM, highlighting their similarities in expense ratios and key differences in diversification, dividend yield, and index methodology. While VB offers broader diversification, SPSM provides a higher dividend yield and higher quality exposure through its profitability screening. The choice between the two depends on investor preference for diversification versus yield and quality.

feed.title: Telecom Argentina Stet - France Telecom S.A. (NYSE:TEO) Receives Consensus Rating of "Hold" from Analysts
feed.summary: Telecom Argentina Stet - France Telecom S.A. (NYSE:TEO) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from five research firms, with an average one-year target price of $13.87. JPMorgan Chase & Co. recently upgraded TEO to "Overweight" with a $16 price target, while other analysts provided mixed ratings. The company recently reported $0.02 EPS and has a market capitalization of $5.17 billion.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp shareholders approve all proposals at annual meeting
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ:SYBT) announced that shareholders approved all proposals at its 2026 annual meeting, including the election of all nominated directors and the ratification of BDO USA, P.C. as the accounting firm. A non-binding resolution for executive compensation was also approved. This follows recent reports of core pre-provision net revenue slightly below estimates and a raised price target from Stephens.

feed.title: Selling pressure pushes Dollar General stock lower in today's trading
feed.summary: Dollar General Corporation (DG) saw its stock fall by 2.18% to $113.34, trading below its key moving averages, which indicates persistent bearish pressure. Experts from Traders Union note that all momentum and sentiment indicators point to a strong downtrend, with Anton Kharitonov highlighting the lack of supportive news and potential for further downside if $110.56 fails. Conversely, Viktoras Karapetjanc suggests the current volatility could offer buying opportunities for forward-looking investors if sentiment shifts positively.

feed.title: Wells Fargo (WFC) issues $430,000 notes; 5.20% coupon, callable 2029
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company is offering $430,000 in Fixed Rate Callable Medium-Term Notes, Series AA, with a 5.20% annual coupon. These notes, due April 30, 2041, are callable by Wells Fargo semi-annually starting April 30, 2029. The offering price is $1,000 per note, with potential variations for institutional investors, and the notes are senior unsecured obligations not FDIC insured or exchange-listed.

feed.title: Paychex Inc. Stock (US7043261079): Q3 2026 Earnings Beat Estimates on Strong Payroll Growth
feed.summary: Paychex Inc. (NASDAQ: PAYX) announced strong Q3 fiscal 2026 results, surpassing revenue and EPS expectations due to robust demand from U.S. small businesses for payroll and HR services. The company reported $1.32 billion in revenue, a 4.2% year-over-year increase, and adjusted EPS of $1.25, exceeding forecasts. Paychex reaffirmed its full-year guidance, highlighting its stable business model, dividend growth streak, and strong position in the U.S. payroll processing market.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America Stock (US6951561022): Reports Strong Q1 2026 Results
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America (NYSE: PKG) has reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with revenue reaching $2.0 billion, a 5% increase year-over-year, largely due to strong containerboard demand. The company also announced adjusted EPS of $2.10, surpassing consensus estimates, and a net income of $190 million for the quarter ending March 31, 2026. This performance highlights the company's resilience in the packaging sector, driven by demand from e-commerce and food industries.

feed.title: Starbucks Shares Advance After Earnings Top Expectations, Analysts See More Upside Ahead
feed.summary: Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) shares rose after the company reported better-than-expected Q1 earnings, with adjusted EPS of 50 cents against an estimated 43 cents and revenue of $9.53 billion against an estimated $9.15 billion. The company also raised its fiscal year adjusted EPS guidance and analysts increased their price targets on the stock. Technical indicators suggest continued bullish momentum for SBUX.

feed.title: CoStar Group (NASDAQ:CSGP) Sets New 12-Month Low Following Analyst Downgrade
feed.summary: CoStar Group (NASDAQ:CSGP) shares hit a new 52-week low after Needham & Company LLC lowered its price target from $60 to $50, and other analysts also trimmed their targets, creating selling pressure. Despite beating Q1 earnings expectations with $0.23 EPS and 23% revenue growth, raising FY2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance, and authorizing a $1.5 billion share buyback, the stock traded down about 6.2%. Investor concerns over Homes.com spending, AI risks, and the stock's high trailing P/E ratio contributed to the decline.

feed.title: Suncor Energy stock price forecast: C$91.00 resistance tests as SU adds 1.69%
feed.summary: Suncor Energy Inc. (SU) gained 1.69% today, trading at C$90.64 and remaining above its key moving averages. Analysts anticipate year-over-year earnings growth for the March 2026 quarter, driven by higher revenues, though some overbought indicators suggest short-term caution. The stock is expected to consolidate between C$89.00 and C$93.50, with a high probability of further gains if it breaks above C$91.00.

feed.title: Live Nation's $30 concert ticket sale begins today. Here's a list of DC-area shows included
feed.summary: Live Nation is launching its "Summer of Live" concert series, offering $30 tickets to thousands of shows nationwide, including many in the D.C. area. The sale, which began today, April 29th, 2026, features artists like Wu-Tang Clan, Pitbull, Tim McGraw, and TLC & Salt-N-Pepa. The offer ends on May 5th, 2026, and tickets are expected to sell out quickly.

feed.title: General Mills faces setback in supply chain sustainability push
feed.summary: General Mills experienced a setback in its supply chain sustainability efforts in fiscal 2025, with total greenhouse gas emissions reduction falling to 14% from 19% in the prior year, against a 2020 baseline. The company also saw increased water usage per ton of finished product and struggled to meet animal welfare standards for broiler chickens. However, all manufacturing facilities achieved zero-waste-to-landfill status, and 95% of packaging was designed to be recyclable or reusable.

feed.title: Take-Two CEO Reveals GTA 6 Price Strategy and Hints at Return of L.A. Noire
feed.summary: Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick discussed the company's pricing strategy for games like GTA 6, emphasizing that consumers pay for value and that the price charged should be fair for the experience received. He also hinted that Rockstar Games has not forgotten about legacy IP like L.A. Noire, and that their teams are always considering such titles for potential future development. GTA 6 is set to launch on November 19 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

feed.title: Tenable faces earnings test as AI pivot meets sequential decline
feed.summary: Tenable Holdings Inc. is set to report its first-quarter earnings, facing scrutiny on whether its AI-powered exposure management strategy can counteract a projected sequential decline in earnings and revenue. Analysts have a largely positive outlook despite a recent downgrade, with a consensus "Buy" rating and significant upside potential. Investors will closely watch committed remaining performance obligations (cRPO) and the company's ability to monetize its AI initiatives, especially after a strong fourth-quarter performance that beat expectations.

feed.title: Franklin Resources raised to Hold-equivalent at Barclays after Q2 financial results (BEN:NYSE)
feed.summary: Barclays upgraded Franklin Resources (BEN) to a Hold-equivalent rating following the release of its second-quarter financial results, citing improving fundamentals. The asset manager's shares rose 2% after the market opened after reporting FQ2 non-GAAP EPS of $0.71, beating analyst estimates by $0.16.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories Stock: Why Analysts Rate a Buy in 2026
feed.summary: Despite a recent stock decline of 22.5% year-to-date and tempered full-year 2026 guidance, Abbott Laboratories (ABT) is largely rated a "buy" by analysts due to its underlying business strength and strategic acquisitions. The Exact Sciences deal, adding the Cologuard test, is expected to layer significant incremental revenue, while its medical devices segment continues strong growth. Analysts anticipate that post-dilution, the combined portfolio will drive accelerating earnings growth from 2027 onwards, with a TIKR model suggesting a mid-case target of $154.

feed.title: BMO Capital Adjusts Price Target on CMS Energy to $85 From $84, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BMO Capital has increased its price target on CMS Energy (NYSE: CMS) to $85 from $84, while reaffirming an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst movements, including Barclays lowering its target and Morgan Stanley adjusting theirs. CMS Energy recently reported beating profit estimates for Q1 2026 and boosted its capital expenditure plan.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: FirstEnergy tops Q1 2026 forecasts, stock dips
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Corporation (FE) reported Q1 2026 earnings per share of $0.72 and revenue of $4.2 billion, both exceeding analyst forecasts. Despite these strong results, the company's stock declined by 1.59% in premarket trading. FirstEnergy reaffirmed its full-year 2026 core earnings guidance and highlighted its strategic investments in regulated businesses and ongoing cost management efforts.

feed.title: Teradyne Q1 2026 slides: record AI-driven results meet skeptical market
feed.summary: Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) reported record-breaking Q1 2026 results, with revenue of $1.282 billion and non-GAAP EPS of $2.56, both exceeding analyst expectations, largely driven by AI-related demand. Despite the strong performance and nearly 70% of revenue being AI-driven, the stock plunged over 18% in premarket trading due to investor concerns about the sustainability of AI growth, premium valuations, and Q2 guidance indicating a sequential revenue decline. The company's long-term outlook projects sustained AI-driven growth, aiming for $6 billion in revenue and $9.50-$11.00 non-GAAP EPS in an expanded ATE market.

feed.title: Vanguard (RMD) reports 11.96M-share stake — 8.21% disclosed
feed.summary: Vanguard Capital Management has reported a significant stake in ResMed Inc. (RMD), holding 11,961,706.2 shares, which represents 8.21% of the common stock as of March 31, 2026. This disclosure was made via a Schedule 13G filing, indicating a passive investment intent rather than an attempt to control the company. Vanguard maintains sole voting power for a portion of these shares and sole dispositive power over the entire beneficial ownership.
